mission.title: Install and Configure Security Compliance on Kubernetes
mission.description: Zot is a production-ready vendor-neutral OCI image registry that allows you to store and manage container images in a compliant manner. Installing Zot will enable you to have a reliable and scalable solution for your container image storage needs.

steps.title: Create a Namespace for Zot
steps.description: First, create a dedicated namespace for the Zot registry to keep it isolated from other applications.

steps.title: Apply the Zot Deployment
steps.description: Next, apply the Zot deployment manifest to set up the registry.

steps.title: Apply the Zot Service
steps.description: Then, apply the service manifest to expose the Zot registry.

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description: Check if the Zot pods are running successfully.

uninstall.title: Remove Zot from the Cluster
uninstall.description: To uninstall Zot, delete the deployment and service, and then remove the namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Zot
upgrade.description: To upgrade Zot, fetch the latest version of the deployment and service manifests and apply them again.

troubleshooting.title: Pod CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Zot pod is in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors using:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n zot
```

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Zot service, ensure that the service type is correctly set (e.g., LoadBalancer or NodePort) and check the service status:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n zot
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are not scheduling, check for resource availability. Use:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n zot
``` to see if resource limits are causing issues.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Zot will have all pods in the 'zot' namespace running and accessible via the specified port. You should also be able to configure resource limits and access the service through port-forwarding.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with it.
